description: Baseline: 75 structural diffs across 10 books. Martyr=0, 1984=0, ThreeBelow=3, HeatedRivalry=7, Elvis=6, ThroneOfGlass=5, SunriseReaping=8, Familiars=13, SecretsCrown=16, HungerGames=17

asi.hypothesis: baseline measurement
asi.breakdown: Martyr=0, 1984=0, ThreeBelow=3, HeatedRivalry=7, Elvis=6, ThroneOfGlass=5, SunriseReaping=8, Familiars=13, SecretsCrown=16, HungerGames=17
asi.missing_files_all_from: SecretsCrown (22 missing - likely image directory scanning issue or resource gap)

description: Fix </body> placement: single self-closing elements now have </body> on same line matching lxml serialization. Reduced from 75 to 71 structural diffs (-4).

asi.books_improved: HungerGames 17→16, Familiars 13→12, SunriseReaping 8→7, ThreeBelow 3→2, Elvis 6→5, HeatedRivalry 7→6, ThroneOfGlass 5→4
asi.hypothesis: Fix closing </body> placement for single self-closing HTML elements to match lxml compact serialization
asi.fix: In sectionXHTML, detect single self-closing HTML body content and put </body> on same line

description: Fix JXR image conversion: set correct MIME type (image/jxr) for JXR resources with empty mime field. parseResourceFragment was incorrectly setting image/jpeg, preventing JXR→JPEG conversion. 71→60 structural diffs, 22→0 missing files.

asi.bug: parseResourceFragment set JXR mediaType to image/jpeg instead of image/jxr when mime was empty. This prevented the JXR→JPEG conversion in buildResources from detecting JXR resources.
asi.fix: Set correct MIME type per format: jxr→image/jxr, jpg→image/jpeg, png→image/png
asi.impact: SecretsCrown: -11 structural (JXR refs→JPG), 22 missing→0 (JXR image files now exist as JPG)
asi.image_diffs_increase: 127→138 because JXR images now produce different JPEG encoding vs Calibre

description: Refine </body> placement: only compact for self-closing elements (ending '/>') and SVG. Non-self-closing single-line elements get standard newline before </body>. Fixes 1984 regression (7 diffs→0).

asi.bug: Previous fix put </body> on same line for ALL single-line HTML, not just self-closing elements. This broke pages with <a>, <div>, etc.
asi.fix: Narrow condition: only self-closing elements (ending with '/>') or SVG properties get compact </body>. Non-self-closing elements get standard \n before </body>.
asi.impact: 1984: 7→0 (was incorrectly getting same-line </body>), but kept the image-only page fix for HungerGames/Familiars/etc

description: Fix promoted image body: convert -kfx-box-align to text-align and remove width. 53→17 structural diffs (-68%). HungerGames 17→1, Familiars 13→0, Elvis 5→0, SunriseReaping 1→0, ThreeBelow 1→0.

asi.hypothesis: Promoted image body inherits width:100% from container, preventing class split. Convert box-align→text-align and remove width from body to match Python's create_container behavior.
asi.key_insight: Python converts -kfx-box-align to text-align in create_container (yj_to_epub_content.py:1335-1336). Go's promoted body bypasses this. Also width must be removed from body because Python keeps it on child img, not on body.
attempted_approaches: Full non-heritable property filtering → regressed Martyr by 54 diffs due to class ordering
Only box-align conversion → didn't split classes because width still shared
Targeted: box-align→text-align + delete width → correct, no regression

description: Fix promoted body inline text whitespace: sectionXHTML no leading \\n for plain text + BodyInlineText flag for non-heading \\n prefix. HeatedRivalry 7→3, total 17→13.

attempted_approaches: Adding \n to Root.Children → normalizeHTMLWhitespace converts to
Modifying BodyHTML in renderStoryline → overlay merge overwrites it
Adding \n prefix in processSection → materializeRenderedSections recomputes BodyHTML
asi.hypothesis:
For promoted body inline text, Python's lxml serializes text directly after <body> (no leading 
). For headings, body.text = 'text' directly. For non-headings, body.text = '
text' (from <span> child element unwrapping). sectionXHTML must not add leading 
 for inline text, and non-heading inline text needs 
 prefix in BodyHTML.
asi.key_insight:
Two-part fix needed: (1) sectionXHTML omits leading for plain text, (2) non-heading promoted inline text gets prefix in materializeRenderedSections via BodyInlineText flag (not in Root.Children because normalizeHTMLWhitespace converts to
)

description: Regenerate Calibre reference EPUBs from current code + remove stale BodyInlineText flag. HeatedRivalry 0 structural, 1984 8 (new baseline).

asi.baseline_change: Regenerated all Calibre reference EPUBs from current REFERENCE/Calibre_KFX_Input code. Old references were from an outdated Calibre version with different whitespace handling. New references reflect current Python source of truth.
asi.changes: HeatedRivalry 3→0 (sectionXHTML inline text fix + fresh refs). 1984 0→8 (new diffs from updated reference: trailing whitespace, div wrapper, page anchors). Martyr 0→0 structural but 0→5 image (new image diffs from updated reference).
asi.hypothesis: Updated Calibre reference EPUBs to match current REFERENCE/Calibre_KFX_Input code. Removed stale BodyInlineText flag. HeatedRivalry now at 0 structural diffs.

description: Fresh baseline: regenerated Calibre refs from current Python code. 18 structural diffs across 4 books.

asi.books: Martyr=0, Elvis=0, SunriseReaping=0, SecretsCrown=4, 1984=8, ThreeBelow=0, Familiars=0, HungerGames=1, ThroneOfGlass=5, HeatedRivalry=0
asi.hypothesis: Fresh baseline with regenerated Calibre references from current REFERENCE/Calibre_KFX_Input code

description: Block-aware trailing \\n in sectionXHTML: only add \\n before </body> for block elements. 1984 8->5.

asi.books_improved: 1984 8->5 (3 whitespace diffs fixed by block-aware trailing \n)
asi.hypothesis: Python's beautify_html adds trailing \n only for block elements. sectionXHTML must match: add trailing \n before </body> only when content ends with a block element closing tag, not for inline elements like <a>.

description: Verified: block-aware trailing \\n with /> check is needed. 15 structural diffs stable.

asi.hypothesis: bodyEndsWithBlockElement needs both closing block tags AND self-closing /> tags. Removing either causes regressions.
asi.lesson:
The self-closing /> check catches ,
,
elements. Without it, pages ending with these elements lose trailing \n and regress.

description: Attempted margin auto for box-align → cascading class reorder regression. Reverted.

asi.hypothesis: Attempted to add margin-left/right auto for box-align conversion. This changes the body CSS which cascades to reorder ALL generic classes in the style catalog, causing massive regressions in other books.
asi.key_insight: Any change to the body CSS properties changes the style catalog class ordering. The box-align→text-align + width removal was carefully balanced. Adding margin auto unbalances it. Must find a different approach.
asi.lesson: Body CSS changes have cascading effects on class numbering. Only minimal targeted changes are safe.

description: stripBareDivs: strip bare div wrappers from single-child bodies after style catalog. Fixes SecretsCrown xQ10 (-1 diff, 4→3). 1984 c1K4 also fixed but c9 regressed (net 0 for 1984). Total: 15→14 structural diffs.

asi.hypothesis: Python consolidate_html strips bare divs when parent has exactly 1 child. Go stripBareDivs implements this after style catalog.
asi.fix: stripBareDivs in yj_to_epub_properties.go after beautifyHTML. Only strips when Root has exactly 1 child, div has no attrs, no text, no block children.
asi.impact: SecretsCrown 4→3 (xQ10 fixed). 1984 c1K4 fixed but c9 regressed (same count). SunriseReaping/HungerGames unchanged.
asi.caveat: 1984 c9: Calibre keeps the div but stripBareDivs strips it. c1K4: Calibre strips the div. Both are bare divs with linked images. The difference is in the KFX data — different node structures produce different Python behavior even though the output divs look identical.
asi.key_insight: Python's consolidate_html condition: len(parent)==1 AND parent.tag in block_set → strip. For body: len(parent)==1 AND no text AND no block children → strip. The single-child check is critical.

description: Fix HungerGames c791: don't add \n around htmlText in renderHTMLParts. Two inline images now on same line matching Calibre. HungerGames 1→0 structural diffs. Total: 14→13.

asi.fix: renderHTMLParts: skip \n around htmlText parts. lxml serializes text/tail without extra newlines between inline elements. Go was adding \n between all parts including inline text glue between <img/> elements.
asi.hypothesis:
The 
 between inline images in c791 BodyHTML came from renderHTMLParts adding 
 between ALL parts when multiline=true. By skipping 
 around htmlText parts (which are inline text content), the images are serialized on the same line.

description: CSS post-processing: add margin auto to figure rules with width. Fixes 1984 stylesheet diff. 1984 5→4. Total: 13→12 structural diffs.

asi.fix: finalizeStylesheet CSS post-processing: add margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto to .figure_ rules that have text-align AND width properties. Matches Python's create_container box-align→margin auto conversion. Properties are re-sorted alphabetically after insertion.
asi.hypothesis: Adding margin auto as CSS post-processing (not in style map) avoids class ordering cascade while fixing the stylesheet diff.
asi.key_insight: Must check for 'width:' property — not all figure rules should get margin auto. Only figures with explicit width (like image bodies) need it.

description:
Fix position anchor offset arithmetic: count
as 1 char in locateOffsetIn. Python runs locate_offset before \n→
conversion (56 chars), Go after (54 chars). Also swap normalizeHTMLWhitespace/applyPositionAnchors order. 1984 4→1 (cDT page_134 + nav/toc refs fixed). Total: 12→9.

asi.books_improved: 1984 4→1 (cDT page_134 fixed, nav/toc page_134 references fixed)
asi.fix:
locateOffsetIn: count
elements as consuming 1 offset character. Python runs locate_offset BEFORE replace_eol_with_br ( →
conversion), so offsets include chars. Go runs splitTextHTMLParts ( →
) during rendering, BEFORE locateOffset. The
counting compensates for the removed characters.
asi.hypothesis:
Position anchors fail at end-of-content because \n→
conversion reduces text length. Counting
as 1 offset character restores the correct offset arithmetic.
asi.key_insight:
Python's locate_offset runs on text WITH (56 chars). Go's locateOffset runs on text WITHOUT (54 chars, 2
elements). Each
represents 1 removed char. Counting
as consuming 1 offset makes Go's offset arithmetic match Python's.
asi.also_swapped: normalizeHTMLWhitespace and applyPositionAnchors order (applyPositionAnchors first now). This matches Python's order but alone wasn't sufficient because splitTextHTMLParts already removed \n during rendering.

description: Skip promoted body for figure containers with font-size. Python's COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS keeps <div> wrapper when styles overlap with body defaults. Go now replicates this by checking container font-size. ToG c4D fixed. Total: 9→8.

asi.fix: Skip promotedBodyContainer for figure-hinted resource nodes that have font-size in their container style. Python's COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS keeps the <div> wrapper when body and container share CSS properties (like font-size). Without font-size, the <div> merges into <body>. With font-size, it stays and gets converted to <figure> by simplify_styles.
asi.hypothesis: Figure-hinted image containers with font-size should NOT be promoted to body — their <div> wrapper should be kept for figure conversion by simplify_styles.
asi.key_insight: Python's COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS checks if content_style ∩ child_style is empty (except style-name). If container has font-size (which body defaults also have), the intersection is non-empty → don't merge → <div> stays → converted to <figure>.

description: Fix ToG cM rendering: block promotedBodyContainer Case 2 inline promotion when BOTH template container and child node have position anchor IDs. Python's COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS won't merge inner div into template when both have id attributes, so Go must also not promote inline. Fixes Throne of Glass (4 diffs → 0), reduces total from 8 → 4.

asi.hypothesis: ToG cM renders inline because promotedBodyContainer Case 2 always promotes single text nodes. Python's COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS blocks merging when both parent and child have id attributes (from position anchors). Go should check the same condition.
asi.root_cause: Template container for cM has position anchor 'page_v', child node has 'id__212_67_Dedication'. COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS: (id in parent.attrib and id in child.attrib) → don't merge. Go's promotedBodyContainer didn't check this.
asi.fix: Added check in renderStoryline: when inline=true and template has position anchor, also check if child node has position anchor. If both have anchors → ok=false → don't promote inline.
asi.remaining_diffs: SC: class_220-0/1 ordering swap (2 xhtml + 1 CSS), class_93-0 extra font-size. 1984: c9 bare div stripped by stripBareDivs but kept by Calibre.
asi.investigation_method: Python COMBINE_NESTED_DIVS debug logging revealed parent/child id attribute check as the blocking condition

description: Fix SC drop cap rendering: split dropcap events at sub-event boundaries and fix sort order. Python's find_or_create_style_event_element calls split_span when a later event overlaps a dropcap, copying float style to BOTH halves. Go's applyAnnotations now splits dropcap events at other events' boundaries, creating separate float spans for each sub-segment. Also fixes sweep sort to put annotation (outer) before dropcap (inner) when they share the same range. Fixes SC xQ213 + xQ875 (class_220-0/1 ordering). Total: 4→2 structural diffs.

asi.fix: 1) Added dropcap field to event struct. 2) After collecting events, split dropcap events at boundaries of other overlapping events. 3) Sort non-dropcap before dropcap at same start/end for correct outer/inner nesting.
asi.hypothesis: Split dropcap events at sub-event boundaries to match Python's split_span behavior. Sort annotation before dropcap at same position for correct nesting.
asi.pre_existing_check_failure: TestBuildBookStateFragmentSummaryMatchesReference fails on both HEAD and working tree - not related to this change
asi.remaining_diffs: SC class_93-0 extra font-size (1 CSS diff), 1984 c9 bare div (1 HTML diff)
asi.root_cause: Go created 1 float span per dropcap section (covering both quote and letter), Python created 2 (one per character after split_span). This caused different style counts → different class ordering in catalog.

description: Fix SC drop cap rendering: split dropcap events at sub-event boundaries and fix sort order. Python's find_or_create_style_event_element calls split_span when a later event overlaps a dropcap, copying float style to BOTH halves. Go's applyAnnotations now splits dropcap events at other events' boundaries, creating separate float spans for each sub-segment. Also fixes sweep sort to put annotation (outer) before dropcap (inner) when they share the same range. Fixes SC xQ213 + xQ875 (class_220-0/1 ordering). Total: 4→2 structural diffs.

asi.books_improved: SecretsCrown 3→1 (xQ213 and xQ875 now structurally identical)
asi.fix: 1) Added dropcap bool field to event struct. 2) After collecting all events, scan dropcap events for overlapping sub-events and split at boundaries. 3) Sort non-dropcap before dropcap at same start/end for correct outer/inner nesting (annotation wraps dropcap, matching Python's replace_element_with_container).
asi.hypothesis: Split dropcap events at sub-event boundaries to match Python's split_span behavior
asi.key_insight: Python's locate_offset → split_span copies parent style to both halves when a later event overlaps a dropcap span. This creates 2 float spans per section instead of 1, doubling the float style count in the catalog and fixing class_220-0/1 assignment.
asi.remaining_diffs: SC class_93-0 extra font-size (1 CSS diff), 1984 c9 bare div (1 HTML diff)
asi.root_cause: Go created 1 float span covering both quote and letter in dropcap. Python created 2 separate spans (one per sub-segment after split_span). Different counts → different frequency-based class ordering.

description: Improve audit tool: cross-file matching finds 73 more function matches. Python class methods are often implemented in different Go files than the matching filename would suggest. 362→289 missing functions.

asi.hypothesis: Cross-file matching in audit tool finds functions that exist in other Go files
asi.key_insight: Many Python methods on classes like KFX_EPUB_Properties are implemented in Go in different files (e.g. fixAndQuoteFontFamilyList is in yj_to_epub_content.go). The audit tool now checks all Go files.
